name: Venison Jerky
recipeIngredient: A few grains of cayenne pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Freeze venison until firm and solid enough to slice easily.
2. Cut 1/8-inch slices with a sharp knife or slicer, then cut the slices into strips that are 1 & 1/2 inches wide.
3. In a bowl, combine the remaining ingredients and pour over the venison strips that have been arranged in rows in a shallow baking pan. Marinate overnight in the refrigerator.
4. Drain well.
5. Dehydrator: Cover trays with strips without overlapping. Dry for 4 hours at 140°F. Turn the strips and rotate the trays. Dry another 6 to 8 hours. Well-dried jerky should be dark and fibrous, looking brittle enough to splinter when bent in two.
6. Sun drying is not recommended in most climates.
7. Oven: Lay strips of marinated meat in rows over trays, being careful not to overlap the strips. Dry at 110°F until the edges will splinter when bent in two, which takes 18 to 24 hours.
